clientConfigPage.title: Client Setup
clientConfigPage.subtitle: Write the reverse proxy or a third-party provider into AI client configs in one click (auto-backup, revertible).
clientConfigPage.empty: No profiles for this client yet
clientConfigPage.emptyHint: Click "Add Profile" to write a provider into its config
clientConfigPage.coexistHint: Multiple providers can be injected at once — account & third-party models coexist
clientConfigPage.switchHint: Only one provider is active at a time; switching overwrites
clientConfigPage.relayInjectionHint: When on, Codex connects only to the 号小管 proxy; account + third-party models are aggregated in /model (true coexistence). When off, providers switch individually.
clientConfigPage.accountProviderHint: Use your account quota via the local reverse proxy
clientConfigPage.emptyThirdParty: No third-party providers yet — click "Add provider"

form.previewHint: Enter a URL to preview the config to be written
form.previewRelayNote: On enable, the URL is rewritten to the local reverse proxy
form.modelMapHint: Set the actual model per tier; display name only affects the /model menu; tick 1M to declare long context. Blank tiers are skipped
form.thirdPartyHint: Enter a third-party provider URL & key (direct). For the reverse proxy, use "Connect local proxy".
form.name: Name
form.upstreamProtocolHint: If it differs from this client's protocol, traffic is converted & relayed via the local proxy
form.routingForcedHint: This upstream protocol differs from the client's; traffic must be converted & relayed via the local proxy.
form.routingHint: When on, third-party traffic is relayed via the proxy (account pool / IP). The API service auto-starts on enable if it's not running.
form.codexModelListHint: Generates the Codex model_catalog_json for the /model menu. Changes take effect after restarting Codex.

clientConfigPage.codexSwitchDesc: After switching to "{{name}}", older sessions may become invisible in Codex due to provider filtering. Enable the toggle below to migrate history sessions to the new provider so they remain visible.
clientConfigPage.codexSwitchRepairToggle: Also repair history sessions (migrate to new provider)

clientManage.title: Client Manager
clientManage.subtitle: Detect each AI client CLI's installed version, update to latest in one click, and diagnose multi-install conflicts for the same command
clientManage.conflictTitle: Multiple installations detected (update only affects the PATH-default one)

service.disabledHint: Enable the service to use these endpoints

clientKeys.title: Client Keys
clientKeys.emptyHint: Create a key to use it in the Authorization Bearer header
clientKeys.plaintextWarning: This key will not be shown again. Copy it now.

poolHealth.title: Account Pool Health
poolHealth.emptyHint: Add Kiro accounts in Account Management to see runtime state here

sessionsView.subtitle: Conversation history from local AI tools
sessionsView.emptyDetail: Select a session to view details
sessionsView.emptyDetailSub: Click any session from the left panel
sessionsView.emptyListSub: Sessions will appear here after using this AI tool
sessionsView.confirmDeleteDesc: This action cannot be undone. Delete this session?
sessionsView.confirmDeleteBatchDesc: This will permanently delete {{count}} selected sessions.
sessionsView.repairExplain: Codex filters sessions by the current provider. When you switch providers, older sessions become invisible in Codex. Repairing merges sessions to the current provider, making them visible again.
sessionsView.repairCodexRunning: Codex is running. It will be safely stopped before repair and restarted afterward.
sessionsView.repairEncryptedNote: Encrypted sessions remain visible after switching providers, but continuing conversations may be limited by upstream restrictions.
sessionsView.repairDone: Repaired {{n}} sessions. Changes take effect after Codex restarts.
sessionsView.repairUnavailable: No repairable Codex session library detected
sessionsView.unsupportedSub: Session browsing for this client coming soon

faq.title: FAQ
items: How do new users authorize an account?
Error <System temporarily unavailable, please retry later.>
Why can't Bilibili-Open authorize a new account?

support_panel.title: Join the community
support_panel.desc: Scan to join our group for help and updates.

update.title: Update available
update.ready: The update is downloaded. Click "Install now" to restart and apply it.
